dateline: Tampa, Florida — Prosecutors Reject HYBE Chair's Arrest Warrant; BTS Opens North America Leg Tomorrow
heroHeadline: Bang Si-hyuk Arrest Warrant Rejected; BTS Tampa Opener Tomorrow Draws Beatlemania Comparisons
heroSubtitle: 4,698 days since debut: Seoul prosecutors rejected the arrest warrant for HYBE Chairman Bang Si-hyuk on April 24, citing insufficient evidence for detention — a major legal development three days after police filed the warrant over alleged ₩260B IPO fraud. Meanwhile, BTS's ARIRANG World Tour North America leg opens at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa tomorrow (April 25), with US media comparing the fan frenzy to Beatlemania. 180,000 fans are expected across three sold-out nights. 'SWIM' has topped the Billboard Global 200 for four consecutive weeks.
footerNote: Data sourced from HYBE, Billboard, Hanteo Chart, Gaon Chart, Melon, Spotify, Netflix, Guinness World Records, IFPI, Korea Times, Korea Herald, Yonhap, Bloomberg, Variety, AP News, UPI, Starnews Korea, Rolling Stone, PBS NewsHour, Euronews, City of Tampa, Business Observer Florida, WTSP Tampa Bay, FOX 13 Tampa Bay, Tampa Bay Times, Sportskeeda, and Recording Academy.
